notes.content: DATED MATERIAL, SUBJECT TO CHANGE. PLEASE CONSULT CURRENT UCSD GENERAL CATALOG FOR ANY ADDITIONAL INFORMATION. Effective Fall 2018, major preparation will be required for this major. For details, visit: http://admissions.ucsd.edu/MajorPrep Effective Fall 2015, the B.S. in Computer Science, the B.S. in Computer Engineering, and the B.S. in Computer Science with a specialization in Bioinformatics and the B.S. in Artificial Intelligence are impacted for transfer students. Visit cse.ucsd.edu for full information. General advice: Transfer students are advised to complete the following courses for their major before enrolling at UC San Diego. Preparing well for the major helps students move efficiently toward graduation. The following is a list of all required lower-division courses for the major: - Calculus I-for Science and Engineering (Math. 20A) - Calculus II-for Science and Engineering (Math. 20B) - Calculus and Analytic Geometry (Math. 20C) - Linear Algebra (Math. 18) - One course chosen from: PHYS 2A or PHYS 4A, PHYS 2B or PHYS 4B, CHEM 6A or CHEM 6AH, CHEM 6B or CHEM 6BH, BILD 1, BILD 2, BILD 3 - Introduction to Programming and Computational Problem Solving (CSE11) Basic Data Structures and Object-Oriented Design (CSE 12) Discrete Mathematics (CSE 20) Course equivalency: For course equivalencies not listed below, visit the CSE Student Affairs Office, CSE Building (EBU3B, Room 1200) first floor, or email csestudent@.ucsd.edu. For information not found here, please visit the CSE Undergraduate Program at: https://cse.ucsd.edu/undergraduate
notes.contentHtml:
DATED MATERIAL, SUBJECT TO CHANGE. PLEASE CONSULT CURRENT UCSD GENERAL CATALOG FOR ANY ADDITIONAL INFORMATION.


Effective Fall 2018, major preparation will be required for this major. For details, visit: http://admissions.ucsd.edu/MajorPrep


Effective Fall 2015, the B.S. in Computer Science, the B.S. in Computer Engineering, and the B.S. in Computer Science with a specialization in Bioinformatics and the B.S. in Artificial Intelligence are impacted for transfer students. Visit cse.ucsd.edu for full information.


General advice: Transfer students are advised to complete the following courses for their major before enrolling at UC San Diego. Preparing well for the major helps students move efficiently toward graduation. The following is a list of all required lower-division courses for the major:


  1. - Calculus I-for Science and Engineering (Math. 20A)
  2. - Calculus II-for Science and Engineering (Math. 20B)
  3. - Calculus and Analytic Geometry (Math. 20C)
  4. - Linear Algebra (Math. 18)
  5. - One course chosen from: PHYS 2A or PHYS 4A, PHYS 2B or PHYS 4B, CHEM 6A or CHEM 6AH, CHEM 6B or CHEM 6BH, BILD 1, BILD 2, BILD 3
  6. - Introduction to Programming and Computational Problem Solving (CSE11)
  7. Basic Data Structures and Object-Oriented Design (CSE 12)
  8. Discrete Mathematics (CSE 20)


Course equivalency: For course equivalencies not listed below, visit the CSE Student Affairs Office, CSE Building (EBU3B, Room 1200) first floor, or email csestudent@.ucsd.edu.


For information not found here, please visit the CSE Undergraduate Program at: https://cse.ucsd.edu/undergraduate
